name: When We Return
description: Khoj and Goethe-Institut / Max Mueller Bhavan New Delhi are pleased to announce the opening of ‘When We Return’, Six curated exhibitions by CISA 2025 Fellows. The exhibition presents the final culmination of CISA’s seventh edition, which brought together curators from Bangladesh, India, Iran, Nepal and Sri Lanka for a two weeks-long, fully-funded, in-situ research […]

name: April by David Yambem
description: A woman in her late twenties, residing and working in a city, takes a respite and returns home, which is situated in a fictional town in Manipur. After an extended period of separation from her home, she eagerly anticipates rekindling the connection with her roots. A representation of many migrant’s longing for home and also […]

name: Luidwang (Unwithering)
description: The music video launch of Luidawng by Lamhoiching and Jacob is a tribute to an idea of home through old Paite folk songs. Having been internally displaced from Manipur, both the artists reflect on the yearning for a return to home. The work also stands as a blessing to the youth, to remain like an […]

name: Fundamentals of Arts Management | Lucknow
description: A short course by Khoj International Artists’ Association led by Pooja Sood & Ruchira Das. From 13 to 15 March, 2026 (10:30 am to 5:30 pm) at Lucknow Bioscope in Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh. The 3-day course on “Fundamentals of Arts Management” will cover the key aspects of arts management: strategic planning, marketing, financial planning, and […]

name: Only in Humayunpur : A Public Art Display
description: In a place already filled with art in the form of graffiti this is a public display of works by two artists who have had long associations with Humayunpur. This attempt is at bringing the distant and exclusive act of Art viewing into these streets, where people often do not have the time, or perhaps […]

name: The Lunchbox
description: Hosted as part of the public programming for as needed, Screening: The Lunchbox brings together an afternoon of viewing and conversation around the film’s themes of intimacy, urban life, and the quiet politics of everyday exchange. The screening will be followed by a discussion exploring the film and its broader thematic resonances with the exhibition.

name: The Body as Site: Cruelty, Hunger, and the Politics of Memory
description: The Body as Site: Cruelty, Hunger, and the Politics of Memory brings together Agustina Andreoletti and Mukesh Kulriya for a podcast conversation exploring how the body carries histories of violence, erasure, and resilience. The session reflects on the body as a living archive—residue and vibration—shaped by social, political, and institutional forces, yet still capable of […]
